aliases: Callistemon viminalis (former botanical name, still widely used)
Chorisia speciosa (former botanical name, still widely used in horticulture)
Jatropha pandurifolia (former botanical name, still widely used)
Jacaranda acutifolia (former name, still seen in older sources)
(also sold as Ruellia brittoniana, R. tweediana, R. caerulea)
(hybrids often sold as Canna x generalis)
Senecio confusus (former botanical name, still widely used)
Also called golden fig, higueron, or simply strangler fig. Florida's Native peoples are documented to have used the latex sap for chewing gum and the stems and roots for tools, cordage, and medicine.
Star Jasmine (a name shared with other plants)
